description: Work 'wepuri' when it moves towards a named stage or criterion. The criterion matters because movement alone may lead away from what people now need. 'helui' (change) is broader, while 'sholu' (complete) says that the required parts or steps are present.
articulatory_notes: For voiced 'w', round the lips while lifting the tongue back, then release into unrounded true-mid front 'e'. Seal the lips for unvoiced 'p' and open into stressed high-back rounded 'u'. In final 'ri', the tongue tip taps the alveolar ridge once or trills there briefly before high-front 'i'.
sound_symbolism: The first two syllables echo 'wepu' (go), so progress has an audible kinship with movement. Final 'ri' changes the cadence through a brief ridge contact and bright close.
usage_notes: The verb is intransitive, and its event noun is progress. The surrounding account can identify the undertaking, stage, or criterion. A change of plan may also change what counts as progress.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A project can test progress against repair, access, ecological effect, or human need instead of treating activity and growth as sufficient evidence.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: A stated criterion keeps progress contingent on present conditions rather than turning time itself into a promise of improvement.

semantic_domains.activity: movement towards a selected practical stage or criterion
semantic_domains.cognition: a comparison between present work and an intended state
semantic_domains.temporal: change traced across stages of an undertaking
